items.misconception: Three separate words run together by mistake.
items.random_wrong: It is too long to be a real word.
items.why: Students think long words are typos. German freely COMPOUNDS nouns into single words: Donau + Dampf + Schiff + Fahrt + Gesellschaft. The last element determines the gender and the core meaning.

A devastating faux ami. Bekommen looks like 'become' but means RECEIVE. 'Ich bekomme ein Geschenk' = I receive a present. To become = WERDEN. Confusion: 'I became a present' instead of 'I received a present'.

items.q: Why can you not just ignore umlauts in German?
items.correct: They change the pronunciation AND meaning of words.
items.random_wrong: They are pronounced the same as without dots.
items.why: Students treat umlauts as optional accents. They are CHANGED VOWELS. Schon (already) vs schön (beautiful) are different words. Mutter (mother) vs Mütter (mothers) are singular vs plural.
The answer examiners want is: A returnable deposit. “A waste tax” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: When was the Klimaschutzgesetz first passed?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 2019. “2009” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What percentage emission reduction by 2030 does the revised Climate Act target?
items.why: The real figure is 65. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
